message:
fix(daemon): use CTRL_BREAK_EVENT for graceful Windows shutdown (bd-20)

On Windows, SIGTERM is not supported and always fails, causing
sendStopSignal to immediately fall back to TerminateProcess (Kill).
This interrupts in-flight git commits and DB writes with no chance
for graceful shutdown.

Replace the broken SIGTERM+Kill approach with
GenerateConsoleCtrlEvent(CTRL_BREAK_EVENT), which is the standard
Windows mechanism for graceful process termination. The daemon is
already started with CREATE_NEW_PROCESS_GROUP, so its group ID
equals its PID. The CTRL_BREAK_EVENT is received as os.Interrupt
by the daemon signal handler, triggering a clean shutdown.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Executed-By: beads/polecats/pearl
Rig: beads
Role: polecats
